name: Gingersnap Gravy
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Black pepper                      1/2 ts Dried thyme leaves
1/2 tsp White pepper                     1/4 ts Ground cayenne pepper
1/2 tsp Ground ginger                    1/8 ts Ground cumin
recipeInstructions:
------------------------------MAIN INGREDIENTS------------------------------
2 tbsp Chicken, pork, or beef fat 6 cups Basic chicken stock
2 tbsp Unsalted butter 1 cup Pan drippings from chicken
3/4 cup Finely chopped onions 8 pieces Gingersnap cookies
1/2 cup Finely chopped celery 1 tsp Light brown sugar, to taste
1/2 tsp Minced garlic 1 tsp Ground ginger, to taste
Combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside. Melt
the fat and butter in a large skillet over medium heat. When almost
melted, add the onions, celery, and garlic; saute for 5 minutes, stirring
occasionally. Stir in the seasoning mix and cook for an additional 5
minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the stock and pan drippings; bring to a
boil over high heat and boil rapidly until the liquid reduces to about 1
quart, approximately 25 minutes. Then crumble the gingersnaps into the
stock mixture and whisk with a metal whisk until they are fully dissolved.
Continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes, whisking frequently and
ensuring that the gingersnaps are completely dissolved. During this time,
taste the gravy; if the ginger flavor is not pronounced, add the 1 tablespoon
brown sugar and 1 teaspoon ground ginger to taste. Strain the gravy.
Makes 2-1/2 to 3 cups.
From Paul Prudhomme's "Louisiana Kitchen"
